history.qwen_observation: Tests fail with NullPointerException on serviceMock because Mockito fails to initialize @Mock fields on JDK 21, likely due to an outdated Mockito version incompatible with JDK 21's reflection/module restrictions.
history.qwen_rationale: Adding a JUnit 4 to 5 migration step will upgrade Mockito to a JDK 21-compatible version (5.x) and replace @RunWith(MockitoJUnitRunner.class) with @ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class), ensuring mocks are properly initialized. This step is inserted after the Java 11 migration to allow the test framework to stabilize before further JDK upgrades.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed due to missing JUnit 4 and JUnit 5 migration support dependencies after the JUnit 4→5 migration step, causing cannot find symbol errors for org.junit.Rule and org.junit.jupiter.migrationsupport.rules.
history.qwen_rationale: Injecting JUnit 4, JUnit 5, and migration support dependencies via a POM patch ensures the classpath is complete before the migration recipes run and before plugin upgrades trigger compilation. Upgrading Mockito to 5.x simultaneously resolves JDK 21 compatibility issues for mock initialization.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed at upgrade_plugins_for_java17 because JUnit 4 classes (org.junit.Rule, org.junit.runner.RunWith) were missing from the test classpath, likely removed or overridden during the JUnit 4→5 migration step.
history.qwen_rationale: The JUnit4to5Migration recipe often cleans up JUnit 4 dependencies after migrating code, causing subsequent compilation steps to fail when test sources still reference JUnit 4 APIs or when Mockito 5.x requires explicit JUnit 4 coordinates. Using force_version ensures existing JUnit/Mockito version tags are updated to compatible versions, and add_dependency guarantees JUnit 5 and Mockito JUnit Jupiter are present. Placing this patch before the migration ensures the classpath is stable for both the migration recipe and the following plugin upgrade.

history.qwen_observation: The upgrade_plugins_for_java17 step failed because JUnit 4 classes (org.junit.Rule, org.junit.runner.RunWith, org.junit.rules.ExternalResource) and JUnit 5 migration support (org.junit.jupiter.migrationsupport.rules) are missing from the test classpath — the pom_patch step used force_version on junit but never added the junit:junit dependency itself.
history.qwen_rationale: The force_version op only rewrites existing version tags; it does not add missing dependencies. The tests use JUnit 4 annotations (@Rule, @RunWith) and JUnit 5 migration support classes, so both junit:junit:4.13.2 and junit-jupiter-migrationsupport:5.10.2 must be explicitly added as test dependencies. Adding mockito-core:5.14.2 as well ensures the Mockito classpath is complete. With these dependencies present, the code will compile under JDK 11, allowing the JUnit4to5Migration recipe to run and transform the test code before the Java 17 plugin upgrade.
